reviewers.name: codex-adversarial-diff-review
reviewers.focus: Correctness, races, cache-invalidation ordering, data loss on replace, backward compat, migration safety on ~507K-row transcriptions, test adequacy.
reviewers.finding: 2 blockers (live-meeting Redis hash deletion via PATCH; merge->rename correction loss across mode=replace), 3 majors (running-export staleness race, meeting.data JSONB lost update, schema-sync non-CONCURRENTLY index build), 1 minor (stale speaker filter after merge).

resolution_summary: All 6 findings fixed in the same working tree with regression tests: post-meeting-only 409 gate before any mutation; speaker_corrections.aliases map keeps merge groups durable through later renames and replace; rerun_requested flag + pre-done meeting refresh closes the mid-export race and the export/PATCH JSONB clobber; SELECT FOR UPDATE serializes concurrent PATCHes; Index info online_only makes schema-sync skip the synchronous build (migration script owns CONCURRENTLY creation); dashboard clears the speaker filter after a successful update.
required_follow_up: Record hash-bound human approval for diff sha256:75f30469dbe6e6127afcc651ff1a51078e2abf576368125534a27a92e08b8f37 before PR.
Run scripts/migrations/20260708_add_speaker_cluster.py up on production BEFORE deploying meeting-api.
Record a real Soniox async request/response golden once SONIOX_API_KEY is provisioned (current diarization golden is synthetic; within-file speaker-number stability to be validated per P0 note).
Phases 2-4 (#25-#27) remain separate sub-plans with their own contracts and approvals.
